name: Zadkiel
description: Zadkiel, whose name means 'Righteousness of God,' is a prominent archangel within Jewish, Christian, and Islamic angelology. He is often associated with the virtue of mercy and is considered one of the chief angels, frequently identified as one of the nine angels who rule Heaven. Zadkiel is particularly revered in Kabbalistic traditions, where he is linked to the sephirah Chesed (Mercy), the fourth of the ten emanations on the Tree of Life. He is often depicted bearing a knife, a reference to the Akedah narrative, where he is said to have stayed Abraham's hand from sacrificing Isaac. Zadkiel embodies divine justice tempered with compassion, representing the potential for redemption and the importance of forgiveness. His role as an intercessor and guide highlights the balance between divine law and divine grace within Abrahamic theological frameworks. He is also associated with memory and is invoked for assistance in recalling information or past experiences. Zadkiel's multifaceted nature underscores his significance as a celestial figure embodying core ethical and spiritual principles.
subtitle: Angel of Mercy

keyMyths.title: The Binding of Isaac
keyMyths.description: Zadkiel is most famously associated with the biblical account of the Akedah, or the Binding of Isaac. When Abraham, in an act of profound faith, was about to sacrifice his son Isaac at God's command, it was Zadkiel who intervened, staying Abraham's hand with a knife. He revealed that it was a test of Abraham's devotion, and a ram was provided as a substitute sacrifice. This story highlights Zadkiel's role as a messenger of divine mercy and a protector from unnecessary suffering.

keyMyths.title: Angel of Prayer and Remembrance
keyMyths.description: In some traditions, Zadkiel is considered the angel of prayer, particularly those prayers seeking forgiveness and understanding. He is also linked to memory and the ability to recall past events, both personal and historical. This association stems from his role in reminding humanity of God's covenants and promises. He is invoked for assistance in remembering important information, accessing forgotten skills, and understanding the lessons of the past.

keyMyths.title: Guardian of Freedom
keyMyths.description: Zadkiel is also seen as a guardian of freedom, both spiritual and physical. He is believed to help individuals break free from limiting beliefs, negative patterns, and oppressive situations. This association is rooted in his role as an angel of mercy, offering redemption and a path towards liberation. He inspires individuals to embrace their true potential and live authentically, free from the constraints of fear and doubt. He is sometimes invoked for help in overcoming addictions and other forms of self-destructive behavior.
keyMyths.source: Angel Therapy Handbook by Doreen Virtue

sources.author: Davidson, Gustav
sources.work: A Dictionary of Angels, Including the Fallen
sources.description: Comprehensive dictionary of angels, providing detailed information on Zadkiel's roles and attributes.
